text:
Soffman (ゾフマン, Zofuman) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 205) is a player of the Greed Island game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 133 

Appearance

Soffman has fair, slicked-back hair. He wears a t-shirt over a striped long-sleeved shirt and baggy pants with black shoes. 

Later in the series, he has a bandage over his right eye. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 183 

Plot

Greed Island arc

After Gon and Killua obtain their first specified slot card "Sword of Truth" in the city of Antokiba, Soffman appears with some other experienced players such as Asta and Kazsule with the intention of stealing it. 

Eventually, he's shown to be still alive in the game as there is a bright dot next to his name in Gon's contact list. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

A bruised Soffman is among the players who teleports to Gon, Killua, and Biscuit before Greed Island's quiz begins. When Killua asks him if he's there to sell them the final card if he wins the quiz, Soffman seems uneasy and says it's something like that, which confuses Killua. 

As soon as the 100-question quiz was over, Gon won it. Gashta and Zetsk Bellam arrive at the location, and thank Soffman for sticking around for them. 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Soffman is capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Soffman's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 
Soffman's original Japanese name is written as ゾワマン (Zowaman) in the Hunter's Guide databook, probably an error/typo since the katakana フ (fu) is visually very similar to ワ (wa). 

Anime and Manga Differences

Soffman is only shown from behind in the 2011 anime adaptation. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 75 (2011) 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, though he is among the players who teleport to Gon's group following their acquisition of 99 specified slot cards, Soffman working as a scout for the Bellam Brothers is not depicted.
